auth_rules.redis_required: token must also exist in Redis to be valid

prompts.name: shared-types
prompts.description: Shared TypeScript types for the auth domain in packages/types

prompts.name: user-schema
prompts.description: Verify and complete the Mongoose User schema
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read apps/api/src/auth/schemas/user.schema.ts and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing field, decorator, or export. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The User schema already exists at apps/api/src/auth/schemas/user.schema.ts with fields: role, email, username, clinicId, passwordHash. Review it and apply only the following improvements if they are not already present:

1. Add `select: false` to the passwordHash field so it is never returned in queries by default.
2. Add `immutable: true` to the role field so it cannot be changed after creation.
3. Add compound sparse indexes:
   - unique index on { role: 1, email: 1 } for non-attendant roles
   - unique index on { clinicId: 1, username: 1 } for attendants

Do NOT recreate the schema from scratch. Only add what is missing. Keep all existing fields and decorators intact.

prompts.name: clinic-professional-schemas
prompts.description: Verify and complete Mongoose schemas for Clinic, Professional and ClinicProfessional
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read each of the listed files and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing field, decorator, index, or export. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The following schemas already exist and are largely complete:
- apps/api/src/clinics/schemas/clinic.schema.ts — Clinic with name and subscriptionStatus (enum: active|inactive|trial, default: trial)
- apps/api/src/professionals/schemas/professional.schema.ts — Professional with specialty and userId (unique ObjectId ref User)
- apps/api/src/professionals/schemas/clinic-professional.schema.ts — ClinicProfessional with clinicId and professionalId, unique compound index already present

Review each schema and apply only the following improvements if they are not already present:
1. clinic.schema.ts: ensure SubscriptionStatus enum values are exactly: active | inactive | trial, default is trial.
2. professional.schema.ts: ensure userId has unique: true.
3. clinic-professional.schema.ts: ensure the compound index { clinicId: 1, professionalId: 1 } is unique.

Do NOT recreate any schema from scratch. Only add what is missing.

prompts.name: users-module
prompts.description: UsersModule with repository, service and bcrypt password hashing
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files listed and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing class, method, import, or module registration. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The User schema already exists at apps/api/src/auth/schemas/user.schema.ts — import it from there. Do NOT create a new schema.
The Role enum already exists at apps/api/src/common/enums/role.enum.ts — import it from there. Do NOT recreate it.

Create apps/api/src/users/users.repository.ts with:
- @Injectable() class UsersRepository
- constructor injects @InjectModel(User.name) model
- create(data: Partial<User>): Promise<UserDocument>
- findByEmailAndRole(email: string, role: Role): Promise<UserDocument | null> — uses .select('+passwordHash') to include the hash
- findByClinicIdAndUsername(clinicId: string, username: string): Promise<UserDocument | null> — uses .select('+passwordHash')
- findById(id: string): Promise<UserDocument | null>

Create apps/api/src/users/users.service.ts with:
- @Injectable() class UsersService
- constructor injects UsersRepository
- hashPassword(plain: string): Promise<string> — bcrypt with cost factor 12
- comparePassword(plain: string, hash: string): Promise<boolean>
- createUser(dto: { role: Role; email?: string; username?: string; clinicId?: string; password: string }): Promise<UserDocument>
  - attendant role requires username and clinicId, must not have email
  - non-attendant roles require email, must not have username or clinicId
  - hashes password before saving
  - throws ConflictException on duplicate (MongoDB error code 11000)
- findByEmailAndRole(email: string, role: Role): Promise<UserDocument | null>
- findByClinicIdAndUsername(clinicId: string, username: string): Promise<UserDocument | null>
- getById(id: string): Promise<UserDocument>

Create apps/api/src/users/users.module.ts:
- imports MongooseModule.forFeature with User schema from apps/api/src/auth/schemas/user.schema.ts
- provides UsersRepository and UsersService
- exports UsersService and UsersRepository

Update apps/api/src/app.module.ts: register UsersModule only if it is not already registered. Do not remove or modify any existing module registrations. The file already imports: RedisModule, AuthModule, UsersModule, ClinicsModule, ProfessionalsModule, ClinicProfessionalsModule.

prompts.name: signup-and-login
prompts.description: Signup and login endpoints with DTOs and auto-login after signup
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files listed and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing class, method, DTO, import, or decorator. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The following DTOs already exist — do NOT recreate them:
- apps/api/src/auth/dto/login-standard.dto.ts — LoginStandardDto with email and password
- apps/api/src/auth/dto/login-attendant.dto.ts — LoginAttendantDto with clinicId, username, and password

The Role enum already exists at apps/api/src/common/enums/role.enum.ts — import from there.
The User schema already exists at apps/api/src/auth/schemas/user.schema.ts — do NOT recreate it.

Create apps/api/src/auth/dto/signup.dto.ts with SignupDto:
- role: Role (required, @IsEnum(Role))
- email?: string (@IsEmail(), @IsOptional())
- username?: string (@IsString(), @IsOptional())
- clinicId?: string (@IsMongoId(), @IsOptional())
- password: string (@IsString(), @MinLength(8))

Create apps/api/src/auth/auth.service.ts with AuthService:
- constructor injects UsersService, JwtService, RedisService
- signup(dto: SignupDto): Promise<IAuthTokens> — calls usersService.createUser(dto), signs JWT with payload { sub: userId, role }, calls redisService.saveToken(userId, token, 7*24*3600), returns { accessToken }
- loginStandard(dto: LoginStandardDto): Promise<IAuthTokens> — calls usersService.findByEmailAndRole(email, Role.PATIENT), throws UnauthorizedException if not found, calls usersService.comparePassword, throws UnauthorizedException if wrong, signs JWT, saves to Redis, returns { accessToken }
- loginAttendant(dto: LoginAttendantDto): Promise<IAuthTokens> — calls usersService.findByClinicIdAndUsername(clinicId, username), throws UnauthorizedException if not found, calls usersService.comparePassword, throws UnauthorizedException if wrong, signs JWT, saves to Redis, returns { accessToken }
- logout(userId: string): Promise<void> — calls redisService.removeToken(userId)
- JWT secret from ConfigService.get('JWT_SECRET'), expiry '7d'

Create apps/api/src/auth/auth.controller.ts with AuthController:
- POST /auth/signup — public, calls authService.signup(dto), returns 201 with IAuthTokens
- POST /auth/login — public, calls authService.loginStandard(dto), returns 200 with IAuthTokens
- POST /auth/login/attendant — public, calls authService.loginAttendant(dto), returns 200 with IAuthTokens
- POST /auth/logout — protected by @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard), uses @CurrentUser() from apps/api/src/common/decorators/current-user.decorator.ts, calls authService.logout(userId), returns @HttpCode(204)

The @CurrentUser() decorator already exists at apps/api/src/common/decorators/current-user.decorator.ts — do NOT recreate it.

Create apps/api/src/auth/auth.module.ts:
- imports JwtModule.registerAsync using ConfigService for secret and signOptions { expiresIn: '7d' }
- imports PassportModule
- imports UsersModule from apps/api/src/users/users.module.ts
- provides AuthService
- controllers: [AuthController]
- exports AuthService

prompts.name: redis-token-service
prompts.description: Redis service for revocable JWT session management
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files listed and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing class, method, or provider. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

Create apps/api/src/redis/redis.service.ts:
- @Injectable() class RedisService
- constructor injects ConfigService
- private client: Redis (ioredis) — created in constructor using REDIS_URL from ConfigService, fallback to 'redis://localhost:6379'
- saveToken(userId: string, token: string, ttlSeconds: number): Promise<void> — calls this.client.setex('auth:token:{userId}', ttlSeconds, token)
- getToken(userId: string): Promise<string | null> — calls this.client.get('auth:token:{userId}')
- validateToken(userId: string, token: string): Promise<boolean> — gets stored token, returns stored === token
- removeToken(userId: string): Promise<void> — calls this.client.del('auth:token:{userId}')

Create apps/api/src/redis/redis.module.ts:
- @Global() @Module that provides and exports RedisService

The RedisModule is already registered in apps/api/src/app.module.ts — do NOT add it again.

prompts.name: jwt-strategy-and-guards
prompts.description: JWT strategy with Redis dual-validation and guards
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files listed and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing class, method, guard, decorator, or import. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

IMPORTANT — these already exist, do NOT recreate them:
- @CurrentUser() decorator is at apps/api/src/common/decorators/current-user.decorator.ts
- Role enum is at apps/api/src/common/enums/role.enum.ts

Create apps/api/src/auth/jwt.strategy.ts:
- extends PassportStrategy(Strategy) from passport-jwt
- constructor injects ConfigService and RedisService
- super() options: jwtFromRequest: ExtractJwt.fromAuthHeaderAsBearerToken(), secretOrKey: configService.get('JWT_SECRET'), passReqToCallback: false
- validate(payload: { sub: string; role: Role }): Promise<{ userId: string; role: Role }>
  - calls redisService.getToken(payload.sub)
  - if null, throws UnauthorizedException('Session expired or revoked')
  - returns { userId: payload.sub, role: payload.role }

Create apps/api/src/auth/guards/jwt-auth.guard.ts if it does not already exist:
- @Injectable() class JwtAuthGuard extends AuthGuard('jwt')

Create apps/api/src/auth/guards/roles.guard.ts:
- @Injectable() class RolesGuard implements CanActivate
- constructor injects Reflector
- canActivate: reads 'roles' metadata, if none returns true, else checks request.user.role

Create apps/api/src/auth/decorators/roles.decorator.ts:
- export const Roles = (...roles: Role[]) => SetMetadata('roles', roles)

Update apps/api/src/auth/auth.module.ts to add JwtStrategy and RolesGuard to providers if not already present.

prompts.name: auth-tests
prompts.description: Unit and integration tests for auth: hashing, login, JWT, Redis, logout
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files listed and check what is already implemented. Do not duplicate any existing test, describe block, or mock. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

The test files already exist with the following coverage — only add what is missing, do not rewrite what is already there:

aps/api/src/auth/tests/auth.service.spec.ts already covers: signup, loginStandard, loginAttendant, logout — do NOT rewrite it.

apps/api/src/auth/tests/redis.service.spec.ts already covers: saveToken, getToken, validateToken, removeToken — do NOT rewrite it. The RedisService creates its ioredis client in the constructor (not in onModuleInit), so do NOT call onModuleInit() in tests.

apps/api/src/auth/tests/jwt.strategy.spec.ts already covers: validate returns payload when token in Redis, validate throws when token not in Redis — do NOT rewrite it.

apps/api/src/auth/tests/auth.e2e.spec.ts already covers the full flow: signup → login → logout → reject — do NOT rewrite it.

If all four files are already complete and correct, make no changes.
